Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /workspace/project/tb-difficulty-tuner/src/tb_difficulty_tuner/models.py:
     1	"""Data models for task validation, agent runs, and analysis."""
     2	
     3	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
     4	from datetime import datetime
     5	from enum import Enum
     6	from pathlib import Path
     7	from typing import Any
     8	
     9	from pydantic import BaseModel
    10	
    11	
    12	class Difficulty(str, Enum):
    13	    """Task difficulty levels based on agent pass rates."""
    14	    TRIVIAL = "trivial"   # >80% - will be rejected
    15	    EASY = "easy"         # 61-79%
    16	    MEDIUM = "medium"     # 40-60%
    17	    HARD = "hard"         # <40%
    18	    UNKNOWN = "unknown"   # Not yet determined
    19	
    20	
    21	class RunType(str, Enum):
    22	    """Type of run for tracking provenance."""
    23	    DEV = "dev"           # Development/iteration runs (agent tuning)
    24	    PROD = "prod"         # Production validation runs
    25	    VALIDATION = "validation"  # CI/quality validation runs
    26	
    27	
    28	class AuthorType(str, Enum):
    29	    """Who made a change."""
    30	    HUMAN = "human"
    31	    AGENT = "agent"
    32	
    33	
    34	class ChangeType(str, Enum):
    35	    """Type of file change."""
    36	    ADDED = "added"
    37	    MODIFIED = "modified"
    38	    DELETED = "deleted"
    39	
    40	
    41	class Category(str, Enum):
    42	    """Valid task categories."""
    43	    SYSTEM_ADMIN = "system-administration"
    44	    BUILD_DEPS = "build-and-dependency-management"
    45	    DATA_PROCESSING = "data-processing"
    46	    GAMES = "games"
    47	    SOFTWARE_ENG = "software-engineering"
    48	    ML = "machine-learning"
    49	    DEBUGGING = "debugging"
    50	    SECURITY = "security"
    51	    SCIENTIFIC = "scientific-computing"
    52	
    53	
    54	class CICheckResult(BaseModel):
    55	    """Result of a single CI check."""
    56	    name: str
    57	    passed: bool
    58	    message: str
    59	    details: list[str] = []
    60	
    61	
    62	class CIReport(BaseModel):
    63	    """Complete CI validation report."""
    64	    task_path: Path
    65	    timestamp: datetime
    66	    checks: list[CICheckResult]
    67	    all_passed: bool
    68	
    69	    class Config:
    70	        arbitrary_types_allowed = True
    71	
    72	
    73	class TaskMetadata(BaseModel):
    74	    """Parsed task.toml metadata."""
    75	    version: str = "1.0"
    76	    author_name: str
    77	    author_email: str
    78	    difficulty: Difficulty
    79	    category: Category
    80	    tags: list[str]
    81	    expert_time_estimate_min: float | None = None
    82	    junior_time_estimate_min: float | None = None
    83	
    84	
    85	class TaskConfig(BaseModel):
    86	    """Complete task configuration."""
    87	    metadata: TaskMetadata
    88	    verifier_timeout_sec: float = 900.0
    89	    agent_timeout_sec: float = 900.0
    90	    build_timeout_sec: float = 600.0
    91	    cpus: int = 1
    92	    memory_mb: int = 2048
    93	    storage_mb: int = 10240
    94	
    95	
    96	@dataclass
    97	class AgentStep:
    98	    """Single step in an agent's execution trace."""
    99	    timestamp: datetime
   100	    step_number: int
   101	    action_type: str  # e.g., "bash", "file_write", "file_read", "thinking"
   102	    content: str
   103	    result: str | None = None
   104	    duration_ms: int = 0
   105	
   106	
   107	@dataclass
   108	class AgentRun:
   109	    """Complete record of a single agent run."""
   110	    run_id: str
   111	    agent_name: str
   112	    model: str
   113	    task_name: str
   114	    start_time: datetime
   115	    end_time: datetime | None = None
   116	    success: bool = False
   117	    reward: float = 0.0
   118	    exception_type: str | None = None
   119	    exception_message: str | None = None
   120	    steps: list[AgentStep] = field(default_factory=list)
   121	    test_results: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)  # test_name -> "passed"/"failed"
   122	    stdout: str = ""
   123	    stderr: str = ""
   124	    verifier_log: str = ""
   125	
   126	
   127	@dataclass 
   128	class AgentRunSummary:
   129	    """Summary statistics for multiple runs of the same agent."""
   130	    agent_name: str
   131	    model: str
   132	    total_runs: int
   133	    successes: int
   134	    failures: int
   135	    timeouts: int
   136	    pass_rate: float
   137	    avg_duration_sec: float
   138	    test_pass_rates: dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
   139	
   140	
   141	@dataclass
   142	class DifficultyAnalysis:
   143	    """Analysis of task difficulty based on agent runs."""
   144	    task_name: str
   145	    current_difficulty: Difficulty
   146	    measured_difficulty: Difficulty
   147	    agent_summaries: list[AgentRunSummary]
   148	    failure_patterns: list[str]
   149	    near_miss_patterns: list[str]  # Patterns where agents almost succeeded
   150	    difficulty_adjustment_suggestions: list[str]
   151	    is_solvable: bool
   152	    confidence: float  # 0-1 confidence in the analysis
   153	
   154	
   155	@dataclass
   156	class TraceAnalysis:
   157	    """Detailed analysis of agent execution traces."""
   158	    run_id: str
   159	    agent_name: str
   160	    success: bool
   161	    failure_point: str | None  # Where the agent went wrong
   162	    failure_reason: str | None  # Why it failed
   163	    recovery_attempts: list[str]  # What the agent tried to fix the issue
   164	    critical_decisions: list[str]  # Key decision points
   165	    suggestions: list[str]  # How to make task harder/easier based on this trace
   166	
   167	
   168	class CheckOutcome(str, Enum):
   169	    """Outcome of a quality check."""
   170	    PASS = "pass"
   171	    FAIL = "fail"
   172	    NOT_APPLICABLE = "not_applicable"
   173	
   174	
   175	@dataclass
   176	class QualityCheckItem:
   177	    """Single quality check result from Harbor's quality_checker."""
   178	    name: str
   179	    outcome: CheckOutcome
   180	    explanation: str
   181	
   182	
   183	@dataclass
   184	class QualityReport:
   185	    """Complete quality report from Harbor's quality_checker."""
   186	    task_path: Path
   187	    timestamp: datetime
   188	    model_used: str
   189	    checks: list[QualityCheckItem]
   190	    all_passed: bool
   191	    raw_response: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
   192	
   193	
   194	@dataclass
   195	class HarborSummary:
   196	    """Summary report from Harbor's summarizer."""
   197	    job_dir: Path
   198	    timestamp: datetime
   199	    model_used: str
   200	    trial_summaries: dict[str, str]  # trial_name -> summary
   201	    job_summary: str
   202	    summary_file_path: Path | None = None
   203	
   204	
   205	@dataclass
   206	class DifficultyJudgment:
   207	    """Result of LLM judge difficulty validation."""
   208	    task_name: str
   209	    target_difficulty: Difficulty
   210	    judged_difficulty: Difficulty
   211	    difficulty_score: float  # 0.0 (trivial) to 1.0 (hard)
   212	    quality_score: float  # 0.0 to 1.0
   213	    instruction_md_score: float  # 0.0 to 1.0, tokenizer efficiency
   214	    issues: list[str]
   215	    suggestions: list[str]
   216	    passes_target: bool
   217	    confidence: float
   218	    raw_response: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
   219	
   220	
   221	# === Revision Tracking Models ===
   222	
   223	
   224	@dataclass
   225	class FileChange:
   226	    """A single file change in a revision."""
   227	    file: str
   228	    change_type: ChangeType
   229	    lines: str | None = None  # e.g., "15-22" or "45"
   230	    description: str | None = None
   231	
   232	
   233	@dataclass
   234	class TaskRevision:
   235	    """A single revision entry for a task."""
   236	    revision: int
   237	    timestamp: datetime
   238	    author: str  # human name or agent name (e.g., "kirsten", "kimi-k2")
   239	    author_type: AuthorType
   240	    description: str
   241	    changes: list[FileChange] = field(default_factory=list)
   242	    run_type: RunType | None = None  # only for agent changes
   243	    triggering_run: str | None = None  # e.g., "2025-12-24__12-30-15/task__abc123"
   244	    reason: str | None = None  # why the change was made
   245	
   246	
   247	@dataclass
   248	class TaskRevisionHistory:
   249	    """Complete revision history for a task."""
   250	    task_name: str
   251	    task_path: Path
   252	    current_revision: int
   253	    revisions: list[TaskRevision]
   254	
   255	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
   256	        """Serialize to dictionary for JSON output."""
   257	        return {
   258	            "task_name": self.task_name,
   259	            "current_revision": self.current_revision,
   260	            "revisions": [
   261	                {
   262	                    "revision": r.revision,
   263	                    "timestamp": r.timestamp.isoformat(),
   264	                    "author": r.author,
   265	                    "author_type": r.author_type.value,
   266	                    "description": r.description,
   267	                    "changes": [
   268	                        {
   269	                            "file": c.file,
   270	                            "type": c.change_type.value,
   271	                            "lines": c.lines,
   272	                            "description": c.description,
   273	                        }
   274	                        for c in r.changes
   275	                    ],
   276	                    "run_type": r.run_type.value if r.run_type else None,
   277	                    "triggering_run": r.triggering_run,
   278	                    "reason": r.reason,
   279	                }
   280	                for r in self.revisions
   281	            ],
   282	        }
   283	
   284	
   285	@dataclass
   286	class RunMetadata:
   287	    """Metadata attached to a run for provenance tracking."""
   288	    run_type: RunType = RunType.PROD
   289	    revision: int | None = None  # which task revision this tests
   290	    triggered_by: str | None = None  # who/what initiated this run
   291	    change_context: str | None = None  # description of what's being tested
   292
